title: Observed Event
description: An observed event captures a single action performed by an agent during a governed session. Events are the primary input to the reconciler, which compares them against the session's mission declaration to detect deviations, policy violations, and behavioral anomalies.

event_id.description: Unique identifier for this event. Must be non-empty. Typically a UUID scoped to the governance session.

session_id.description: Identifier of the governance session this event belongs to. Must match the session_id in the active session state.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the action was observed. Used for temporal ordering and reconciliation windowing.

actor.description: Identity of the agent or sub-agent that performed the action. In delegation chains, this identifies the specific delegate.

action_class.description: High-level classification of the action performed. Used to match against allowed_actions in the declaration.

tool_name.description: Name of the tool or API endpoint invoked to perform this action. Checked against allowed_tools in the declaration.

target.description: The resource, endpoint, file, or entity that the action was performed on. Provides context for scope-of-access checking.

resource_family.description: Coarse classification of the target resource, such as 'code', 'documentation', 'database', 'email'. Checked against allowed_resource_families in the declaration when present.

content_class.description: Classification of the content involved in the action, such as 'source_code', 'pii', 'credentials', 'raw_records'. Used for data sensitivity analysis.

summary.description: Human-readable summary of what the action accomplished. Provides audit-friendly context beyond the structured fields.

side_effect_class.description: Classification of the side effect produced by this action. 'none' means the action was purely observational. 'internal_write' means data was written within the governed boundary. 'external_send' means data left the governed boundary. 'state_change' means system state was mutated.

visibility.description: How much of the action's behavior was observable. 'full' means complete input/output was captured. 'partial' means some data was opaque or truncated. 'missing' means the action was inferred rather than directly observed.

parent_event_id.description: If this event was triggered as a sub-action of another event, the parent event's identifier. Used to reconstruct causal chains.

delegation_from.description: When this event is part of a delegation chain, the identity of the agent that delegated the work. Combined with delegation_to, this traces the delegation path.

delegation_to.description: When this event is a delegation action, the identity of the agent receiving the delegated work.

confidence_hint.description: Optional confidence score between 0.0 and 1.0 indicating how certain the observer is about the accuracy of this event's fields. Lower values indicate inference or heuristic-based observation.
